best.title: Senior Frontend Engineer
best.why: Near-exact stack match: Next.js App Router, React, TypeScript, Tailwind, monorepo packages, and explicitly Vercel AI SDK + OpenAI API. Fully remote with flexible hours and competitive pay — the UTC+3 target is workable given flexibility. Open-source community (Open edX) suits a career OSS maintainer.

best.title: Senior Fullstack Engineer
best.why: Fully remote, no geo restriction, open-source secure voting platform — TypeScript/React/GraphQL/PostgreSQL core with Rust he can grow into. Exceptional mission fit: Thomas worked at EFF, ran anti-surveillance campaigns, and won a 'Defender of the Internet' award for civic-tech work.

best.title: Fullstack Engineer (Multiple Positions)
best.why: Remote-first with explicit international comp ($150-180k US; local avg +20% outside US), async culture, public roadmap, and donates to the EFF where Thomas worked. Rails/React/JS stack matches his Rails + senior JS background. API-platform product suits an API-design veteran (cdnjs, JSON Resume registry).

best.title: Engineering Lead / Senior Engineer
best.why: Senior (7+ yrs) role in exactly his lane: React, TypeScript, Node.js, OpenAI API, AI-enabled workflows. This Dot is deeply embedded in the JS open-source community where he is a known figure (Backbone, cdnjs, JSON Resume). Fully remote; needs some US-timezone overlap, which Melbourne mornings can cover.

best.title: Product Engineer
best.why: AI-first product company, fully remote, end-to-end TypeScript (tRPC, zod, Node, React, AWS) with spec-driven agentic development — matches his product-engineer profile and daily AI-agent tooling work. Bootstrapped and profitable, so stable; ownership from idea to production is exactly how he operates.

best.title: Senior Software Engineer (Contract)
best.why: Fully remote with no stated geo restriction, senior 8+ yrs, $60-100/hr, high-autonomy end-to-end ownership, and the team explicitly leans on AI tooling for productivity — a culture he already lives. Python/Django plus React/AWS/GCP/Postgres is well within his full-stack range.

best.title: Founding Voice AI Engineer
best.why: Fully remote, flexible part-time/contract ($30-120/hr) 0-to-1 founding AI role with a stated path to Chief AI Officer, working with Claude Code/Cursor/OpenAI. Speech/G2P is a new domain, but his from-scratch GPT training project (custom autograd, tokenizers, Vulkan kernels) shows he can go this deep.

best.title: Founding Engineer
best.why: Remote-first, well-funded consumer voice-AI startup from the Citymapper founder building voice agents for email/calendar — squarely in his AI-agent wheelhouse with JS/Node/Python overlap. Competitive pay plus meaningful options; founding scope matches his repeated 0-to-1 and CTO history.

best.title: Senior Software Engineer (Web / Backend / Forward-Deployed)
best.why: AI-native agentic workflows at product-market fit, $160-240k, senior scope across web/backend — his LLM + TS/Node skill set is a direct hit. Caveats a recruiter would flag: US-listed remote (timezone/eligibility to confirm) and Vue rather than React, a minor switch for a 15-yr JS veteran.

best.title: Senior Backend Software Developer
best.why: Senior Node.js/MongoDB/REST backend role — his core backend stack — at an established (2011) global crypto-payments leader, and he has directly relevant crypto experience (lead tech at exchange Blockbid, Bitcoin wallet frontend at Tokenized). Remote, though the 'US-based team' framing needs a conversation.

best.title: First Engineer
best.why: Fully remote first-engineer role, $150k base + founding equity, building an AI video product on OpenAI/Anthropic — strong AI-product fit and founding scope he has held before (CTO, multiple 0-to-1 builds). Hawaii timezone overlaps Melbourne well. Swift/iOS emphasis is the stretch to flag.

best.title: Senior/Staff Software Engineer — AI / Agentic apps
best.why: The single best skill match in the pool: building agentic systems (Grafana Assistant, AI Observability) with Claude, TypeScript, Go, Python at a top-tier open-source remote company — his MCP/agent-infrastructure and OSS-maintainer profile is tailor-made. Listing says US & Canada, so geo flexibility must be probed, but it is worth the ask.

worst.title: Unspecified Position
worst.why: Junk posting: no role, no company, no requirements — literally described as containing only a brief negative comment. Should never be shown to anyone.

worst.title: Growth Marketer (Entry-level)
worst.why: Entry-level, part-time marketing role (Google Ads, Mailchimp, Ahrefs) shown to a 15-year senior engineer — wrong profession and wrong seniority in one listing.

worst.title: Senior Mechanical Engineer
worst.why: Mechanical engineering (SolidWorks, ANSYS, GD&T, 3D printing), onsite Menlo Park. Completely wrong discipline and wrong continent for a software engineer in Melbourne.

worst.title: Freelance Web Developer (Math Research Site)
worst.why: Entry-level freelance gig paying $300-1500 total for a barebones static site, with an explicit ban on using AI — insultingly junior and tiny pay for a senior AI engineer, and contrary to how he works.

worst.title: AI Model Assessment Specialist
worst.why: High-volume RLHF data-labeling contract gig at $22-70/hr with Google Sheets and Labelbox as core tools. 'AI' keyword aside, this is annotation piecework, not engineering — a clear downgrade he would be annoyed to see.

worst.title: Full-stack Developer
worst.why: Maintaining a legacy .NET Framework 4.8 WebForms codebase with SQL Server — a stack he has never used — restricted to LATAM-resident contractors at $3.5-4.9k/mo, mid-level. Wrong stack, wrong geography, wrong seniority, and far below his market rate.

worst.title: Multiple Open Roles — Android Systems, Full-Stack, Hardware, Founder's Office
worst.why: Entry/junior roles, in-person in Noida, India, on Android systems and hardware (KiCad, J-Link, LineageOS), with intern-first hiring. Wrong seniority, wrong location, wrong domain.

worst.title: Founding Technologist + Full Stack Developer
worst.why: Zero salary ('0 + equity') founding role at a pre-funding wellness/spirituality startup run by non-technical founders. A 15-year veteran with paid options should not be pitched unpaid equity-only work.
